steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The text calls the Wikipedia entries "fictitious" — invented content about subjects that do not exist. The blank completes the phrase "recognized as ___ and deleted," so the word must mean something invented or made-up, matching the tone of the passage's description of deliberate hoaxes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — enhancements: Means improvements or additions that make something better. Fake entries for nonexistent subjects harm Wikipedia's reliability; calling them "enhancements" contradicts the passage's framing of them as harmful hoaxes. ✗
  • Choice B — analyses: Means examinations or evaluations of something real. A fabricated entry for a made-up band is not an analysis of any actual subject. ✗
  • Choice C — fabrications: Means invented falsehoods presented as fact. This precisely describes fictitious entries created by mischievous users — made-up information designed to deceive. ✓
  • Choice D — revelations: Means disclosures of previously hidden truths. A revelation exposes something real; these entries invented something false, which is the opposite. ✗
C is correct.
